signals.headline: The quiet reshaping of how cities fund their mornings
signals.summary: A slow shift in municipal financing is changing who pays for the first hour of city life.
keyTakeaways: Private capital is filling gaps left by slower public budgets.
The change is invisible day to day but compounds over a decade.
Transit and parks are the first to feel it.
signals.whyItMatters: How your city wakes up — transit, parks, light — is being decided in budget rooms most people never see.

signals.headline: On-device models cross the threshold that mattered
signals.summary: Local inference is now good enough to change what ships in the box.
keyTakeaways: On-device models now handle tasks that needed the cloud last year.
Latency and privacy improve as inference moves local.
Battery and memory are the remaining constraints.
signals.whyItMatters: When intelligence runs on the phone, the defaults of every app quietly change.

signals.headline: Why the bond market stopped flinching this week
signals.summary: A week of calm in rates suggests the market has repriced its fears.
keyTakeaways: Volatility fell even as the data stayed mixed.
Investors appear to have absorbed the rate path.
Calm here ripples into mortgages and savings.
signals.whyItMatters: The bond market sets the weather for everyone else's money — quiet weeks are worth noticing.

signals.headline: The studio economy is quietly becoming the main economy
signals.summary: Small independent studios are taking share from the platforms that hosted them.
keyTakeaways: Creators are building durable businesses, not just audiences.
Tools now do what teams used to.
Ownership is shifting back to the maker.
signals.whyItMatters: The line between a hobby, a job, and a company is dissolving for a generation of makers.

signals.headline: Tokyo's new rail line redraws the morning commute
signals.summary: A new connection reshapes how a slice of the city gets to work.
keyTakeaways: The line shortens several cross-town journeys.
Neighborhoods near new stops are already shifting.
Small infrastructure changes move daily life the most.
signals.whyItMatters: Transit decides the shape of a morning more than almost anything else in a city.
